topic: An athlete is asked to switch positions
raw_output:
[story 1]

The coach’s voice, usually a steady anchor, had cracked under the weight of his words. "Marcus, we need you to try playing center for the next match. It’s a change of pace." A cold knot tightened in my stomach, squeezing the air from my lungs. I stared at the polished floor, the familiar rhythm of my own movements suddenly feeling alien and wrong. My hands, usually so sure on the ball, felt clumsy, heavy. Every muscle in my body seemed to seize up, refusing to cooperate with the unfamiliar geometry of the new position. I mumbled something inaudible, my gaze fixed on the scuffed toes of my boots, wishing the ground would swallow me whole.

[story 2]

"Think about it, Leo. It’s for the team's benefit." The words echoed in my ears, hollow and distant. I felt a tremor run through my legs, a strange instability beneath me. When the coach finished speaking, I couldn't meet his eyes. I looked down at my reflection in the darkened window—a stranger with wide, unfocused eyes. A sharp, hot flush crept up my neck. I started pacing the edge of the locker room, my steps quick and uneven, a frantic energy bubbling beneath a surface of forced calm. I wanted to bolt, to disappear into the noise of the hallway, anything to escape the suffocating pressure in this small, sterile space.

[story 3]

"This is just a temporary adjustment, Alex. You'll find your groove." The reassurance sounded thin, brittle. I swallowed hard, the dryness in my throat making the action painful. My breath hitched, shallow and rapid. When the coach gestured toward the center of the court, I felt a sudden, intense wave of nausea wash over me. My vision seemed to narrow, the edges of the room blurring into streaks of harsh light. I gripped the edge of my jersey so tightly my knuckles turned white, my entire body vibrating with an almost unbearable tension that made every nerve ending scream in protest. I just wanted the whistle to blow and everything to stop.
